If the server crashes during startup, Playwright\n77\t  // restarts the `command` and retries the health check — this is the built-in\n78\t  // retry mechanism.  We use the lightweight /health endpoint (registered before\n79\t  // all middleware in app.ts) so readiness checks respond in <1ms regardless of\n80\t  // rate-limit or CSRF state.  It returns 200 once the HTTP server is listening\n81\t  // and the database is reachable (SELECT 1), before feed pollers fire.\n82\t  //\n83\t  // reuseExistingServer:\n84\t  //   - CI (process.env.CI=true): Always start a fresh server for clean state\n85\t  //   - Local development (CI unset/false): Reuse existing dev server to avoid\n86\t  //     startup overhead. Start HTTP server (<1s) — health endpoint becomes available\n  //   5. Start feed pollers in background (fire immediately but async)\n  //\n  // Port conflict detection:\n  // Before starting the server, we check if port 3001 is already in use.\n  // This prevents confusing \"port already in use\" errors during test runs.\n  // The check script exits with code 1 if the port is busy, providing clear\n  // guidance on how to resolve the conflict.\n  //\n  // Timeout (60s) accounts for typical server startup time:\n  // - GTFS data loading and initialization (~1-2s)\n  // - Database migrations (~1s)\n  // - VAPID key generation (~1s)\n  // - OpenTelemetry initialization (~1s)\n  // - HTTP server start (<1s)\n  //\n  // The HTTP server starts BEFORE feed pollers fire, so the health endpoint\n  // responds immediately once the database is reachable. Pollers run in the\n  // background after the server is listening.\n  //\n  // Health-check polling & retry:\n  // Playwright polls the `url` every ~500ms until it receives a 2xx response or\n  // the `timeout` is reached.  If the server crashes during startup, Playwright\n  // restarts the `command` and retries the health check — this is the built-in\n  // retry mechanism.  We use the lightweight /health endpoint (registered before\n  // all middleware in app.ts) so readiness checks respond in <1ms regardless of\n  // rate-limit or CSRF state.  It returns 200 once the HTTP server is listening\n  // and the database is reachable (SELECT 1), before feed pollers fire.\n  //\n  // reuseExistingServer:\n  //   - CI (process.env.CI=true): Always start a fresh server for clean state\n  //   - Local development (CI unset/false): Reuse existing dev server to avoid\n  //     startup overhead. test\n68\t```\n69\t\n70\t## Port Conflicts\n71\t\n72\t### What Causes Port Conflicts\n73\t\n74\tThe \"port already in use\" error occurs when:\n75\t\n76\t1. Conflicts\n94\t\n95\t**Option 1: Stop the existing process**\n96\t\n97\t```bash\n98\t# Find the process using port 3001\n99\tlsof -i :3001\n100\t\n101\t# Kill the process (replace PID with actual process ID)\n102\tkill <PID>\n103\t\n104\t# Now run tests again\n105\tnpx playwright test\n106\t```\n107\t\n108\t**Option 2: Let Playwright reuse the existing server**\n109\t\n110\tIf the existing server is your dev server, let Playwright reuse it:\n111\t\n112\t```bash\n113\t# Just run tests - Playwright will detect and reuse\n114\tnpx playwright test\n115\t```\n116\t\n117\t**Option 3: Use a different port (Not Recommended)**\n118\t\n119\tWhile possible, changing the port requires updates to multiple config files:\n120\t\n121\t```typescript\n122\t// playwright.config.ts\n123\tbaseURL: \"http://localhost:3002\",\n124\twebServer: {\n125\t  url: \"http://localhost:3002/health\",\n126\t  // ...\n127\t}\n128\t```\n129\t\n130\t## Environment Variables\n131\t\n132\t### `TEST_MODE`\n133\t\n134\tSet to `\"true\"` when starting the server:\n135\t\n136\t- Disables rate limiting\n137\t- Disables CSRF protection\n138\t- Uses test-friendly configurations\n139\t- Prevents interference from real feeds during testing\n140\t\n141\t### `CI`\n142\t\n143\tControls `reuseExistingServer` behavior:\n144\t\n145\t- `CI=true` (or `CI=1`): Always start a fresh server\n146\t- `CI` unset: Reuse existing server if available\n147\t\n148\t## Troubleshooting\n149\t\n150\t### \"Port already in use\" error\n151\t\n152\t```bash\n153\t# Find and kill the process\n154\tlsof -i failing:\n164\t\n165\t```bash\n166\t# Check if the server is actually running\n167\tcurl http://localhost:3001/health\n168\t\n169\t# Manually start the server to see errors\n170\tcd ../.. && npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts\n171\t```\n172\t\n173\t### Tests fail with \"connection refused\"\n174\t\n175\tThe server might not be ready:\n176\t\n177\t```bash\n178\t# Check server logs for startup errors\n179\t# Increase timeout in playwright.config.ts if needed\n180\twebServer: {\n181\t  timeout: 600 * 1000, // 10 minutes\n182\t}\n183\t```\n184\t\n185\t## Common Workflows\n186\t\n187\t### user-journeys.e2e.ts\n216\t```\n217\t\n218\t## CI vs Local Behavior Summary\n219\t\n220\t| Aspect | CI (GitHub Actions, Argo) | Local Development |\n221\t|--------|---------------------------|-------------------|\n222\t| `reuseExistingServer` | `false` (always fresh) | `true` (reuse if available) |\n223\t| `workers` | 1 (sequential) | Parallel (CPU count) |\n224\t| `retries` | 2 | 0 |\n225\t| `forbidOnly` | `true` | `false` |\n226\t| Server startup | Every test run | Once, then reused |\n227\t\n228\t## Related Files\n229\t\n230\t- E2E Tests - Local Development Guide\n\nThis guide explains how to run E2E tests locally and handle common scenarios.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Run all E2E tests (starts server automatically)\nnpm run test:e2e\n\n# Run a single test file\nnpx playwright test user-journeys.e2e.ts\n\n# Run tests in headed mode (see browser)\nnpx playwright test --headed\n```\n\n## Server Management\n\n### Automatic Server Startup (Default)\n\nBy default, Playwright automatically starts the dev server for you:\n\n```bash\nnpx playwright test\n```\n\n- **Port conflict detection**: Before starting, Playwright checks if port 3001 is available\n- **Server startup**: If port is free, Playwright starts the server automatically\n- **Health checks**: Playwright waits for the `/health` endpoint to respond before running tests\n- **Automatic cleanup**: Server stops when tests complete\n\n### Reusing an Existing Dev Server\n\nIf you already have a dev server running, Playwright can reuse it:\n\n```bash\n# Terminal 1: Start your dev server\nnpm run dev\n\n# Terminal 2: Run tests (will reuse the running server)\nnpx playwright test\n```\n\n**Benefits of reuse:**\n- Faster test runs (no startup overhead)\n- Better for iterative development\n- Preserves server state between test runs\n\n**When to reuse:**\n- Active development on features\n- Running tests frequently while coding\n- Debugging flaky tests that might be timing-related\n\n**When to start fresh:**\n- CI environments (always fresh)\n- Testing clean startup behavior\n- Isolating state-dependent test failures\n- Final verification before commits\n\n### Forcing a Fresh Server Locally\n\nTo override `reuseExistingServer` and force a fresh server:\n\n```bash\n# Set CI=true to disable reuse\nCI=true npx playwright test\n```\n\n## Port Conflicts\n\n### What Causes Port Conflicts\n\nThe \"port already in use\" error occurs when:\n\n1. A previous test run's server didn't shut down cleanly\n2. You have a dev server already running on port 3001\n3. Another process is using port 3001\n\n### How Port Conflict Detection Works\n\nBefore starting the server, Playwright runs a port check script:\n\n```bash\nnpx tsx helpers/check-port.ts\n```\n\nIf port 3001 is busy, the script:\n- Exits with code 1\n- Shows clear error message\n- Provides resolution options\n\n### Resolving Port Conflicts\n\n**Option 1: Stop the existing process**\n\n```bash\n# Find the process using port 3001\nlsof -i :3001\n\n# Kill the process (replace PID with actual process ID)\nkill <PID>\n\n# Now run tests again\nnpx playwright test\n```\n\n**Option 2: Let Playwright reuse the existing server**\n\nIf the existing server is your dev server, let Playwright reuse it:\n\n```bash\n# Just run tests - Playwright will detect and reuse\nnpx playwright test\n```\n\n**Option 3: Use a different port (Not Recommended)**\n\nWhile possible, changing the port requires updates to multiple config files:\n\n```typescript\n// playwright.config.ts\nbaseURL: \"http://localhost:3002\",\nwebServer: {\n  url: \"http://localhost:3002/health\",\n  // ...\n}\n```\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n### `TEST_MODE`\n\nSet to `\"true\"` when starting the server:\n\n- Disables rate limiting\n- Disables CSRF protection\n- Uses test-friendly configurations\n- Prevents interference from real feeds during testing\n\n### `CI`\n\nControls `reuseExistingServer` behavior:\n\n- `CI=true` (or `CI=1`): Always start a fresh server\n- `CI` unset: Reuse existing server if available\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"Port already in use\" error\n\n```bash\n# Find and kill the process\nlsof -i :3001\nkill <PID>\n\n# Or reuse the existing server\nnpx playwright test\n```\n\n### Server doesn't start within timeout\n\nThe health check might be failing:\n\n```bash\n# Check if the server is actually running\ncurl http://localhost:3001/health\n\n# Manually start the server to see errors\ncd ../.. && npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts\n```\n\n### Tests fail with \"connection refused\"\n\nThe server might not be ready:\n\n```bash\n# Check server logs for startup errors\n# Increase timeout in playwright.config.ts if needed\nwebServer: {\n  timeout: 600 * 1000, // 10 minutes\n}\n```\n\n## Common Workflows\n\n### Iterative Development\n\n```bash\n# Terminal 1: Start dev server once\nnpm run dev\n\n# Terminal 2: Run tests repeatedly (fast, reuses server)\nnpx playwright test user-journeys.e2e.ts\n# ... make changes ...\nnpx playwright test user-journeys.e2e.ts\n# ... make changes ...\nnpx playwright test user-journeys.e2e.ts\n```\n\n### Full Test Suite Verification\n\n```bash\n# Fresh server, clean state (slower but thorough)\nCI=true npx playwright test\n```\n\n### Debugging a Single Test\n\n```bash\n# Run with UI mode for interactive debugging\nnpx playwright test --ui\n\n# Run with headed browser\nnpx playwright test --headed user-journeys.e2e.ts\n```\n\n## CI vs Local Behavior Summary\n\n| Aspect | CI (GitHub Actions, Argo) | Local Development |\n|--------|---------------------------|-------------------|\n| `reuseExistingServer` | `false` (always fresh) | `true` (reuse if available) |\n| `workers` | 1 (sequential) | Parallel (CPU count) |\n| `retries` | 2 | 0 |\n| `forbidOnly` | `true` | `false` |\n| Server startup | Every test run | Once, then reused |\n\n## Related Files\n\n- `playwright.config.ts` - Main test configuration\n- `helpers/check-port.ts` - Port conflict detection\n- `package.json` - Test scripts and dependencies\n","numLines":233,"startLine":1,"totalLines":233}}}
